description:
Tier rules for Salesforce API-version maintenance. Dates and tiers live here, never in code. First matching rule wins, top to bottom. `weight` feeds the staleness score; `currentApiVersion` is bumped 3x/year.

Org-integration dated rules come FIRST (before gradient rules) so they win for integration findings. They are keyed on `soapLogin = true`, which is only set for org-scan integration findings of type 'soap-login' — never for any metadata item (repo or org). json.ts enforces this: metadata components never receive a retirementDate regardless of schedule rule output.

Repo/org metadata gradient (no dates): 'current' (within 3 releases of current, weight 0) · 'behind' (4–9 releases back, weight 0.3) · 'far-behind' (more than 9 releases back, weight 0.6) · 'unknown' (no apiVersion detected). Drift is a hygiene and risk signal — old metadata keeps running, pinned to its version's behavior. 'Behind' = version drift distance from current. Never state or imply that staying on an old metadata version blocks access to new objects, fields, or methods — it generally does not.

Thresholds (3 and 9 releases): defaults — 3 ≈ 1 year, 9 ≈ 3 years of Salesforce releases.

rules.label: Far behind — more than 9 releases from current
